message:
Add syntax highlighting and forms/pickers beads

Complete Master TODO Inventory coverage:
- bd-381: Implement syntax highlighting for code blocks
  - Depends on: bd-2uk (Text type), bd-22q (Theme system)
  - Syntect/tree-sitter integration, 10+ languages
- bd-jbg: Implement forms and picker widgets
  - Depends on: bd-376 (Interactive widgets), bd-yzu (Flex layout)
  - Form fields, validation, file/color/date pickers

Total: 84 beads, 98 edges, no cycles
Section I (Extras) now fully covered.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-30h","title":"Implement Sparkline and Chart widgets","description":"PHASE 7 - EXTRAS: DATA VISUALIZATION

## Background
Sparkline and Chart widgets provide data visualization for metrics, trends, and timeseries. Common requirement for monitoring dashboards and CLI tools.

## Technical Requirements

### Sparkline Widget
- Compact single-line visualization
- Data as Vec<f64> or iterator
- Auto-scaling to min/max range
- Configurable bar character set (▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█)
- Color gradient based on value
- Reference line support (mean, threshold)

### Chart Widget (BarChart, LineChart)
- BarChart:
  - Horizontal/vertical orientation
  - Grouped and stacked modes
  - Labels and value display
  - Color per series
- LineChart:
  - Multiple series support
  - Point markers optional
  - Area fill under line
  - Axis labels and ticks
  - Legend positioning

## Design Rationale
Feature-gated for projects that need visualization. Sparkline is minimal overhead. Full charts use Canvas internally for rendering.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sparkline with auto-scaling
- [ ] Sparkline color gradient
- [ ] BarChart with orientations
- [ ] BarChart grouped/stacked
- [ ] LineChart multi-series
- [ ] LineChart axis rendering
- [ ] Feature gate: charts

 {"id":"bd-35p","title":"Implement Progress and Spinner widgets","description":"PHASE 5 - PROGRESS + SPINNER WIDGETS

## Background
Progress indicators and spinners are essential for agent harness UIs showing tool execution status.

## Technical Requirements

### Spinner Widget

pub struct Spinner {
    frames: &'static [&'static str],
    style: Style,
    label: Option<Text>,
}

// Preset spinner styles
pub mod spinners {
    pub const DOTS: &[&str] = &["⠋", "⠙", "⠹", "⠸", "⠼", "⠴", "⠦", "⠧", "⠇", "⠏"];
    pub const LINE: &[&str] = &["|", "/", "-", "\\"];
    pub const BRAILLE: &[&str] = &["⣾", "⣽", "⣻", "⢿", "⡿", "⣟", "⣯", "⣷"];
    pub const MOON: &[&str] = &["🌑", "🌒", "🌓", "🌔", "🌕", "🌖", "🌗", "🌘"];
}

impl StatefulWidget for Spinner {
    type State = SpinnerState;  // Contains current frame index
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, state: &mut Self::State);
}

impl SpinnerState {
    pub fn tick(&mut self) {
        self.frame = (self.frame + 1) % self.total_frames;
    }
}

### Progress Bar Widget

pub struct ProgressBar {
    ratio: f64,  // 0.0 to 1.0
    style: ProgressStyle,
    label: Option<Text>,
}

pub struct ProgressStyle {
    bar_style: Style,
    fill_style: Style,
    empty_style: Style,
    chars: ProgressChars,  // [filled, partial, empty]
}

// Preset progress styles
pub mod progress_styles {
    pub const BLOCK: ProgressChars = ['█', '▓', '░'];
    pub const LINE: ProgressChars = ['=', '>', ' '];
    pub const DOTS: ProgressChars = ['●', '○', '○'];
}

impl Widget for ProgressBar {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer);
}

### Progress with ETA

pub struct ProgressWithEta {
    current: u64,
    total: u64,
    started_at: Instant,
}

impl ProgressWithEta {
    pub fn ratio(&self) -> f64;
    pub fn eta(&self) -> Duration;
    pub fn elapsed(&self) -> Duration;
    pub fn items_per_second(&self) -> f64;
}

## Tests Required
- [ ] Spinner frame cycling
- [ ] Spinner with label
- [ ] Progress bar ratio rendering
- [ ] Progress bar edge cases (0%, 100%, overflow)
- [ ] Partial fill character selection
- [ ] ETA calculation accuracy
- [ ] Wide character handling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Multiple spinner presets
- [ ] Multiple progress bar presets
- [ ] Customizable styles
- [ ] Accurate ETA calculation
- [ ] Works at any width
- [ ] Unicode spinners work

## Location: ftui-widgets crate","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:31:44.547803785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T22:32:20.218541510Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35p","depends_on_id":"bd-2uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:32:20.218510171Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-376","title":"Implement interactive widgets (List, Scrollbar, Input)","description":"PHASE 5 - INTERACTIVE WIDGETS

## Background
Interactive widgets handle user input and state changes. List provides selectable items, Scrollbar visualizes scroll position, Input accepts text entry.

## Technical Requirements

### List Widget
- Items with optional styling per item
- Single/multi selection modes
- Keyboard navigation (up/down/pageup/pagedown/home/end)
- Highlight style for selected item
- Scroll into view on selection change
- Item filtering/search support

### Scrollbar Widget
- Vertical and horizontal orientation
- Track, thumb, and arrow styles
- Thumb size proportional to content ratio
- Click/drag support (if mouse enabled)
- Auto-hide when content fits

### Input Widget
- Single-line text input
- Cursor positioning with blinking
- Text selection (shift+arrow)
- Clipboard operations (copy/paste)
- Placeholder text when empty
- Password mode (masked input)
- Input validation hooks

## Design Rationale
These widgets encapsulate common interaction patterns. State management follows Elm architecture - widgets emit events, parent handles state updates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List with selection modes
- [ ] List keyboard navigation
- [ ] Scrollbar sizing algorithm
- [ ] Scrollbar orientation support
- [ ] Input cursor management
- [ ] Input text selection
- [ ] All widgets implement StatefulWidget trait
- [ ] Event emission for state changes

## StatefulWidget Trait
pub trait StatefulWidget {
    type State;
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, state: &mut Self::State);
}

+{"id":"bd-381","title":"Implement syntax highlighting for code blocks","description":"PHASE 7 - EXTRAS: SYNTAX HIGHLIGHTING

## Background
Syntax highlighting is essential for code-centric TUI applications. Feature-gated as optional extra.

## Technical Requirements

### Highlighting Engine
- Use syntect or tree-sitter for parsing
- Support common languages: Rust, Python, TypeScript, JSON, YAML, TOML, Markdown
- Theme integration (VS Code themes, base16, etc.)
- Line number gutter option
- Diff highlighting (additions, deletions)

### API Design

pub struct SyntaxHighlighter {
    syntax_set: SyntaxSet,
    theme_set: ThemeSet,
    current_theme: String,
}

impl SyntaxHighlighter {
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    
    /// Highlight code string
    pub fn highlight(&self, code: &str, lang: &str) -> Text;
    
    /// Highlight with line numbers
    pub fn highlight_numbered(&self, code: &str, lang: &str, start_line: usize) -> Text;
    
    /// Set theme
    pub fn set_theme(&mut self, name: &str);
    
    /// Get available themes
    pub fn themes(&self) -> &[String];
}

### Performance Considerations
- Lazy syntax loading
- Incremental re-highlighting for edits
- Cache parsed syntax per file extension
- Background highlighting for large files

## Tests Required
- [ ] Rust code highlights correctly
- [ ] Unknown language falls back gracefully
- [ ] Theme switching works
- [ ] Line numbers align correctly
- [ ] Diff highlighting marks changes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 10+ languages supported
- [ ] 3+ built-in themes
- [ ] Integration with Paragraph widget
- [ ] Performance: < 10ms for 1000 lines

 {"id":"bd-397","title":"Create adversarial tests for escape injection","description":"TESTING - ADVERSARIAL SECURITY TESTS

## Background
FrankenTUI must be secure against untrusted content. Escape sequences in user-provided text could corrupt the terminal or execute malicious sequences. This implements ADR-006 (Sanitize-by-Default).

## Threat Model

### Attack Vectors
1. **Log injection**: Untrusted output contains escape sequences
2. **Title injection**: OSC sequences to change window title
3. **Clipboard injection**: OSC 52 to set clipboard
4. **Cursor corruption**: CSI sequences to move cursor
5. **Mode hijacking**: Enabling raw mode, mouse, etc.

### Example Malicious Inputs
- "\x1b[2J" - Clear screen
- "\x1b[H" - Home cursor
- "\x1b]0;pwned\x07" - Set window title
- "\x1b]52;c;SGVsbG8=\x07" - Set clipboard
- "\x1b[?1049h" - Enter alt screen
- "\x1b[?1000h" - Enable mouse
- "\x1b[31mred\x1b[0m" - Color injection

## Technical Requirements

### Sanitizer Implementation
pub fn sanitize_for_display(input: &str) -> Cow<str> {
    // Fast path: no control characters
    if !input.bytes().any(|b| b < 0x20 || b == 0x7F || b == 0x1B) {
        return Cow::Borrowed(input);
    }
    
    // Slow path: filter control sequences
    let mut output = String::with_capacity(input.len());
    let mut chars = input.chars().peekable();
    
    while let Some(c) = chars.next() {
        match c {
            '\x1b' => skip_escape_sequence(&mut chars),
            c if c.is_control() && c != '\n' && c != '\t' => {
                // Replace with visual representation
                output.push_str(&format!("^{}", (c as u8 + 64) as char));
            }
            _ => output.push(c),
        }
    }
    
    Cow::Owned(output)
}

### Test Categories

#### 1. Escape Sequence Filtering
- [ ] ESC [ ... (CSI) filtered
- [ ] ESC ] ... (OSC) filtered
- [ ] ESC P ... (DCS) filtered
- [ ] ESC _ ... (APC) filtered
- [ ] ESC ^ ... (PM) filtered

#### 2. Control Character Handling
- [ ] NUL (0x00) filtered
- [ ] BEL (0x07) filtered
- [ ] BS (0x08) filtered
- [ ] Tab (0x09) preserved
- [ ] LF (0x0A) preserved
- [ ] CR (0x0D) handled
- [ ] DEL (0x7F) filtered

#### 3. Incomplete Sequence Handling
- [ ] Truncated ESC at end
- [ ] Truncated CSI at end
- [ ] Truncated OSC at end
- [ ] Invalid sequence bytes

#### 4. Raw Passthrough (Explicit Opt-In)
- [ ] raw_text() API exists
- [ ] Loud documentation warning
- [ ] Works for ANSI colors when intended

## PTY Tests

### Test Script
for payload in "${MALICIOUS_PAYLOADS[@]}"; do
    # Run payload through ftui log viewer
    echo "$payload" | ftui-test-harness
    
    # Verify terminal state unchanged
    verify_terminal_state
    
    # Verify no escape sequences in output
    verify_no_escapes
done

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All ESC/CSI/OSC/DCS/APC filtered by default
- [ ] Control characters filtered except tab/newline
- [ ] Fast path for clean input (no allocation)
- [ ] Raw passthrough API documented with warnings
- [ ] PTY tests verify terminal state preserved
- [ ] Fuzz testing for parser robustness

 {"id":"bd-3e8","title":"Implement structured logging with tracing","description":"INFRASTRUCTURE - LOGGING & TRACING

## Background
Comprehensive logging is critical for debugging terminal rendering issues, performance analysis, and understanding execution flow. Must support both development debugging and production diagnostics.

## Technical Requirements

### Tracing Infrastructure
- Use tracing crate for structured, async-aware logging
- Log levels: ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE
- Span-based instrumentation for render pipeline
- Subscriber configuration for:
  - Development: pretty-printed to stderr
  - Testing: captured for assertions
  - Production: JSON structured output (optional)

### Key Instrumentation Points
- Frame lifecycle: start → render → diff → present → complete
- Widget rendering: widget type, area, render time
- Buffer operations: resize, clear, set (at TRACE level)
- Input parsing: raw bytes → parsed events
- State changes: model updates, command execution
- Error conditions: with full context

### Log Format
Development:
  2026-01-31T12:00:00.123Z INFO ftui::render frame_start frame=42 widgets=15
  2026-01-31T12:00:00.125Z DEBUG ftui::diff changed_cells=120 skip_rows=10
  
Testing (structured capture):
  LogEntry { level: INFO, target: "ftui::render", msg: "frame_start", ... }

### Feature Gates
- feature = "tracing" - enables all instrumentation
- feature = "tracing-json" - JSON subscriber for production
- Default: tracing disabled for minimal overhead

## Design Rationale
Terminal UIs are notoriously hard to debug. Structured tracing with spans enables understanding complex render pipelines. Feature-gated to avoid overhead in release builds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tracing subscriber configuration
- [ ] Frame-level span instrumentation
- [ ] Widget render spans with timing
- [ ] Input parsing tracing
- [ ] Error context capture
- [ ] Test log capture mechanism
- [ ] Feature gate documentation
- [ ] Example showing log output

## Performance Budget
- Disabled: zero overhead (compiled out)
- Enabled: < 1µs per span enter/exit

 {"id":"bd-3hy","title":"Define per-module unit test coverage requirements","description":"TESTING - UNIT TEST COVERAGE MATRIX

## Background
Each module in FrankenTUI must have comprehensive unit tests. This bead defines the required test coverage for every crate and module.

## Coverage Requirements by Crate

### ftui-core (≥ 80% coverage)
#### Cell Module
- [ ] CellContent creation from char, grapheme
- [ ] CellContent width calculation (ASCII, CJK, emoji)
- [ ] PackedRgba from RGB, RGBA, named colors
- [ ] PackedRgba Porter-Duff alpha blending
- [ ] PackedRgba opacity scaling
- [ ] CellAttrs bitfield operations
- [ ] CellAttrs merge/override
- [ ] Cell construction and defaults
- [ ] Cell bits_eq correctness
- [ ] Cell continuation sentinel
- [ ] Cell builder methods

#### Buffer Module
- [ ] Buffer creation with dimensions
- [ ] Buffer get/set bounds checking
- [ ] Buffer scissor stack operations
- [ ] Buffer opacity stack operations
- [ ] Buffer clear operations
- [ ] Wide character handling
- [ ] Cell iteration
- [ ] Resize behavior (if supported)

### ftui-render (≥ 75% coverage)
#### Diff Module
- [ ] BufferDiff empty (no changes)
- [ ] BufferDiff single cell change
- [ ] BufferDiff row changes
- [ ] BufferDiff skip unchanged rows
- [ ] BufferDiff wide character changes
- [ ] Diff iteration order

#### Presenter Module
- [ ] ANSI escape sequence generation
- [ ] Cursor movement optimization
- [ ] Color state tracking
- [ ] Attribute state tracking
- [ ] SGR sequence batching
- [ ] Hyperlink OSC 8 handling

### ftui-style (≥ 80% coverage)
- [ ] Style creation and defaults
- [ ] Style merge (cascading)
- [ ] Style patch (override)
- [ ] Style builder pattern
- [ ] StyleSheet define/get
- [ ] StyleSheet compose
- [ ] Named style inheritance

### ftui-text (≥ 80% coverage)
- [ ] Text creation from string
- [ ] Text span appending
- [ ] Text width calculation
- [ ] Text truncation with ellipsis
- [ ] Text line splitting
- [ ] GraphemePool interning
- [ ] GraphemePool refcounting
- [ ] Unicode edge cases (ZWJ, VS15/16)

### ftui-layout (≥ 70% coverage)
- [ ] Rect operations (intersection, contains)
- [ ] Flex horizontal layout
- [ ] Flex vertical layout
- [ ] Flex constraint types (Fixed, Percent, Min, Max, Ratio)
- [ ] Flex gap handling
- [ ] Grid row/column solving
- [ ] Grid cell spanning
- [ ] Grid named areas

### ftui-runtime (≥ 70% coverage)
- [ ] TerminalWriter mode transitions
- [ ] Program/Model lifecycle
- [ ] Cmd execution
- [ ] Event dispatch
- [ ] Subscription handling

### ftui-widgets (≥ 70% coverage)
- [ ] Block border rendering
- [ ] Block title positioning
- [ ] Paragraph text wrapping
- [ ] Paragraph alignment
- [ ] Table column sizing
- [ ] Table row selection
- [ ] List item rendering
- [ ] List selection state
- [ ] Input cursor positioning
- [ ] Input text selection

## Test Quality Guidelines

### Each Test Must
1. Test ONE behavior (single assertion focus)
2. Have descriptive name: test_<function>_<scenario>_<expected>
3. Include edge cases (empty, max, boundary)
4. Be deterministic (no random, no time)
5. Be fast (< 10ms each)
6. Be independent (no shared mutable state)

### Property Testing Requirements
Use proptest for:
- Numeric overflow (widths, coordinates)
- Unicode string handling
- Color calculations
- Layout constraint solving

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test module in each crate
- [ ] Coverage thresholds met per crate
- [ ] Property tests for numeric operations
- [ ] Edge case coverage documented
- [ ] Test naming conventions followed
- [ ] No flaky tests

 {"id":"bd-3mo","title":"Implement markup parsing for styled text","description":"PHASE 4/EXTRAS - MARKUP PARSER (Feature-gated)

## Background
Markup parsing allows writing styled text with inline syntax like [bold]text[/bold] or Rich-style markup. Feature-gated as it's not needed by all apps.

## Technical Requirements

### Markup Syntax Options

#### Option A: BBCode-style
[bold]Bold text[/bold]
[italic]Italic[/italic]
[fg=red]Red text[/fg]
[bg=#00ff00]Green background[/bg]
[link=https://example.com]Click here[/link]

#### Option B: Rich-style (Python Rich compatible)
[bold]Bold[/bold]
[red]Red text[/red]
[on blue]Blue background[/on blue]
[link=url]Link text[/link]
[#ff0000]Hex color[/#ff0000]

### Parser Structure

pub struct MarkupParser {
    style_stack: Vec<Style>,
    current_style: Style,
}

impl MarkupParser {
    /// Parse markup string into styled Text
    pub fn parse(&mut self, input: &str) -> Result<Text, MarkupError> {
        let mut spans = Vec::new();
        let mut current_text = String::new();
        
        for token in self.tokenize(input)? {
            match token {
                Token::Text(s) => current_text.push_str(&s),
                Token::StyleStart(style) => {
                    if !current_text.is_empty() {
                        spans.push(Span::styled(
                            std::mem::take(&mut current_text),
                            self.current_style.clone(),
                        ));
                    }
                    self.style_stack.push(self.current_style.clone());
                    self.current_style = self.current_style.merge(style);
                }
                Token::StyleEnd => {
                    if !current_text.is_empty() {
                        spans.push(Span::styled(
                            std::mem::take(&mut current_text),
                            self.current_style.clone(),
                        ));
                    }
                    self.current_style = self.style_stack.pop().unwrap_or_default();
                }
            }
        }
        
        Ok(Text::from_spans(spans))
    }
}

### Error Handling
pub enum MarkupError {
    UnmatchedTag { tag: String, position: usize },
    InvalidColor { value: String },
    InvalidAttribute { name: String },
    NestedLinkNotAllowed,
}

### Extension: Emoji Shortcodes
Optional: :smile: -> 😀, :rocket: -> 🚀

pub fn expand_emoji(input: &str) -> Cow<str> {
    // Fast path: no colons
    if !input.contains(':') {
        return Cow::Borrowed(input);
    }
    // Replace :name: patterns with Unicode
}

### Integration
// Direct usage
let text = markup!("[bold]Hello[/bold] [red]world[/red]");

// Via Text
let text = Text::from_markup("[bold]Hello[/bold]");

// In widgets
Paragraph::new(markup!("[italic]Styled[/italic] text"))

## Tests Required
- [ ] Bold tag parsing
- [ ] Color name parsing
- [ ] Hex color parsing
- [ ] Nested tags
- [ ] Unclosed tag error
- [ ] Escape sequences (\\[)
- [ ] Empty tags
- [ ] Link with URL
- [ ] Performance: 1000 char markup < 100µs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] BBCode-style syntax supported
- [ ] All style attributes (fg, bg, bold, italic, underline)
- [ ] Link parsing with URL extraction
- [ ] Error handling with positions
- [ ] Escape sequence support
- [ ] Optional emoji expansion
- [ ] Feature gate: markup

## Location: ftui-text crate (feature = "markup")","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:37:44.886882212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T22:37:56.617722024Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mo","depends_on_id":"bd-2uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:37:56.617685796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-cw8","title":"Implement Markdown renderer","description":"PHASE 7 EXTRAS - MARKDOWN RENDERER

## Background
Markdown rendering is essential for agent harness UIs that display documentation, help text, and AI-generated content.

## Technical Requirements

### Supported Markdown Elements
- Headers (h1-h6) with styled text
- Bold, italic, strikethrough, code
- Links (rendered as OSC 8 hyperlinks if supported)
- Code blocks with syntax highlighting (optional)
- Lists (ordered and unordered)
- Blockquotes
- Horizontal rules
- Tables (basic)

### Parser Choice
- Use pulldown-cmark for parsing (battle-tested)
- Convert events to ftui Text/Spans

### Renderer Structure

pub struct MarkdownRenderer {
    theme: MarkdownTheme,
    code_theme: Option<SyntaxTheme>,
    link_style: Style,
    max_width: Option<u16>,
}

pub struct MarkdownTheme {
    h1: Style,
    h2: Style,
    h3: Style,
    // ...
    code_inline: Style,
    code_block: Style,
    blockquote: Style,
    link: Style,
}

impl MarkdownRenderer {
    pub fn render(&self, markdown: &str) -> Text {
        // Parse markdown
        // Convert to styled spans
        // Handle line breaks and wrapping
    }
    
    pub fn render_to_buffer(&self, markdown: &str, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        // Render directly to buffer with area constraints
    }
}

### Widget Integration

pub struct Markdown<'a> {
    content: &'a str,
    renderer: MarkdownRenderer,
}

impl Widget for Markdown<'_> {
    fn render(self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        self.renderer.render_to_buffer(self.content, area, buf);
    }
}

    // Use syntect or tree-sitter
}

 {"id":"bd-fbp","title":"Implement inline mode overlay redraw baseline","description":"PHASE 2.5 - INLINE MODE IMPLEMENTATION (CRITICAL)

## Background
Inline mode is a NON-NEGOTIABLE requirement. The overlay redraw baseline is the correctness foundation that works everywhere, before optimization.

## Technical Requirements (from Plan Section D)

### Overlay Redraw Strategy
1. Save cursor position (DECSC or manual tracking)
2. Move cursor to UI region start
3. Clear UI region only (never full screen!)
4. Render UI frame
5. Restore cursor position (DECRC)

### UI Region Anchoring
- Bottom-anchored (default): UI at bottom, logs scroll above
- Top-anchored (optional): UI at top, logs scroll below
- Configurable height: ui_height parameter

### Log Write Coordination
write_log() must:
1. Suspend UI temporarily
2. Print log line to scrollback region
3. Re-render UI in its anchored position
4. Maintain cursor contract

### Cursor Contract
After any present():
- Cursor is at the correct position per active policy
- UI region is intact
- Scrollback is preserved

### Never Clear Full Screen
The DECRC/ED2 pattern that clears full screen is FORBIDDEN in inline mode.
Only clear the bounded UI region using EL (erase line) or localized ED.

## Implementation

pub struct InlinePresenter {
    ui_height: u16,
    anchor: Anchor,  // Bottom or Top
    cursor_saved: bool,
}

impl InlinePresenter {
    pub fn present(&mut self, buffer: &Buffer, writer: &mut TerminalWriter) {
        // 1. Save cursor
        writer.write_all(b"\x1b7")?;  // DECSC
        
        // 2. Move to UI region
        let (_, term_height) = writer.size();
        let ui_start = match self.anchor {
            Anchor::Bottom => term_height - self.ui_height,
            Anchor::Top => 0,
        };
        writer.move_to(0, ui_start)?;
        
        // 3. Clear UI region only (line by line)
        for row in 0..self.ui_height {
            writer.write_all(b"\x1b[2K")?;  // EL - clear line
            if row < self.ui_height - 1 {
                writer.write_all(b"\n")?;
            }
        }
        
        // 4. Move back and render
        writer.move_to(0, ui_start)?;
        // ... emit diff ...
        
        // 5. Restore cursor
        writer.write_all(b"\x1b8")?;  // DECRC
    }
}

## Tests Required
- [ ] UI renders at correct position (bottom/top)
- [ ] Scrollback is preserved after present()
- [ ] Cursor returns to correct position
- [ ] Log writes don't corrupt UI
- [ ] Resize re-anchors correctly
- [ ] PTY test: verify no scrollback corruption

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Overlay redraw works on all terminals
- [ ] Cursor contract maintained
- [ ] UI region bounded, never full-screen clear
- [ ] Log coordination implemented
- [ ] PTY tests pass

## Performance Budget
- Overlay redraw: < 2ms (acceptable for correctness baseline)

+{"id":"bd-jbg","title":"Implement forms and picker widgets","description":"PHASE 7 - EXTRAS: FORMS AND PICKERS

## Background
Interactive forms and picker dialogs are common UI patterns for configuration, data entry, and selection. Feature-gated as optional extra.

## Technical Requirements

### Form Widget
- Field types: TextInput, Checkbox, Radio, Select, Number
- Validation support with error messages
- Tab navigation between fields
- Submit/Cancel actions
- Vertical or horizontal layout
- Required field indicators

### Form API

pub struct Form {
    fields: Vec<FormField>,
    focused: usize,
    on_submit: Option<Box<dyn Fn(&FormData)>>,
}

pub enum FormField {
    Text { label: String, value: String, placeholder: Option<String> },
    Checkbox { label: String, checked: bool },
    Radio { label: String, options: Vec<String>, selected: usize },
    Select { label: String, options: Vec<String>, selected: usize },
    Number { label: String, value: i64, min: Option<i64>, max: Option<i64> },
}

impl Form {
    pub fn new(fields: Vec<FormField>) -> Self;
    pub fn focus_next(&mut self);
    pub fn focus_prev(&mut self);
    pub fn submit(&self) -> FormData;
    pub fn validate(&self) -> Vec<ValidationError>;
}

### Picker Widgets
- FilePicker: Browse filesystem with filtering
- ColorPicker: Visual color selection
- DatePicker: Calendar-based date selection
- ConfirmDialog: Yes/No confirmation

### Picker API

pub struct FilePicker {
    current_dir: PathBuf,
    filter: Option<String>,  // glob pattern
    show_hidden: bool,
    selection: Option<PathBuf>,
}

impl FilePicker {
    pub fn new(start_dir: impl AsRef<Path>) -> Self;
    pub fn filter(mut self, pattern: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn show_hidden(mut self, show: bool) -> Self;
    pub fn selected(&self) -> Option<&Path>;
}

## Tests Required
- [ ] Form field navigation works
- [ ] Validation errors display correctly
- [ ] Submit collects all values
- [ ] FilePicker navigates directories
- [ ] ColorPicker returns valid RGB

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All form field types implemented
- [ ] Keyboard navigation complete
- [ ] FilePicker with glob filtering
- [ ] ConfirmDialog with Yes/No
- [ ] Accessible focus indicators

 {"id":"bd-wr2","title":"Implement core widgets (Block, Paragraph, Table)","description":"PHASE 5 - CORE WIDGETS

## Background
Core widgets provide essential UI building blocks. Block is a container with borders/title, Paragraph renders styled text with wrapping, Table displays tabular data.

## Technical Requirements

### Block Widget
- Border styles: None, Single, Double, Rounded, Thick, Custom
- Title positioning: Top/Bottom × Left/Center/Right
- Padding (inner margin)
- Style for border and background
- Child content area calculation

### Paragraph Widget
- Text rendering with word/char wrap modes
- Alignment: Left, Center, Right, Justify
- Scroll offset for long content
- Line height/spacing
- Style inheritance from parent

### Table Widget
- Header row with column titles
- Column constraints (width, alignment)
- Row selection/highlighting
- Cell styling
- Horizontal/vertical scroll
- Fixed header during scroll

## Design Rationale
These three widgets cover the majority of TUI use cases. Block is the universal container, Paragraph handles all text display, Table presents structured data.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Block with all border styles
- [ ] Block title positioning
- [ ] Paragraph word wrap
- [ ] Paragraph alignment modes
- [ ] Table column constraints
- [ ] Table row selection
- [ ] All widgets implement Widget trait
- [ ] Style customization for all components
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests

## Widget Trait
All widgets implement:
pub trait Widget {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer);
}

 {"id":"bd-xn2","title":"Create CI/CD pipeline configuration","description":"INFRASTRUCTURE - CI/CD PIPELINE

## Background
Continuous integration ensures code quality, test coverage, and performance budgets are maintained. Must run on every PR and push to main.

## Technical Requirements

### GitHub Actions Workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)

#### Jobs Overview
| Job | Trigger | Purpose | Blocking |
|-----|---------|---------|----------|
| check | PR, push | Format, clippy, tests | Yes |
| coverage | PR, push | Coverage thresholds | Yes |
| benchmarks | push to main | Performance budgets | Warn |
| e2e | PR, push | End-to-end tests | Yes |
| docs | PR, push | Doc build verification | Yes |

#### Check Job
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test --all-features
- cargo test --no-default-features

#### Coverage Job
- Use cargo-llvm-cov for coverage
- Thresholds:
  - Overall: ≥ 70%
  - ftui-core: ≥ 80%
  - ftui-render: ≥ 75%
- Upload to codecov.io

#### Benchmarks Job
- Run on push to main only
- Performance budgets from plan:
  - Cell comparison: < 1ns
  - Present (80×24, 5% changed): p50 < 1.0ms
  - Diff scan: < 100µs
  - Layout solve (20 items): < 100µs
- Fail on 2x regression

#### E2E Job
- Run scripts/e2e_test.sh
- Upload artifacts on failure
- Test on ubuntu-latest and macos-latest

#### Docs Job
- cargo doc --all-features --no-deps
- Verify no broken links
- Upload docs artifact

### Matrix Testing
- OS: ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest
- Rust: stable, nightly
- Features: default, all, minimal

### Caching
- Cache ~/.cargo/registry
- Cache ~/.cargo/git
- Cache target/ with hash key

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] check job with format/clippy/tests
- [ ] coverage job with thresholds
- [ ] benchmarks job with budgets
- [ ] e2e job with script execution
- [ ] docs job with verification
- [ ] Matrix: 3 OS × 2 toolchains
- [ ] Proper caching configuration
- [ ] Badge in README

 {"id":"bd-yzu","title":"Implement Flex layout with constraint solving","description":"PHASE 5 - FLEX LAYOUT

## Background
Flex layout provides 1D constraint-based sizing for rows/columns. Core layout primitive that most complex layouts build upon. Based on CSS Flexbox concepts adapted for terminal grid.

## Technical Requirements
- Flex struct with direction (Row/Column)
- Constraint types:
  - Fixed(u16): exact size
  - Percent(f32): percentage of parent
  - Min(u16): minimum size, grow to fill
  - Max(u16): maximum size, shrink to fit
  - Ratio(u16, u16): proportional sharing
- Constraint solving algorithm:
  1. Allocate Fixed constraints first
  2. Calculate Percent based on remaining space
  3. Distribute remainder to Min/Ratio proportionally
  4. Clamp Max constraints
- Gap/spacing between items
- Cross-axis alignment: Start, Center, End, Stretch

## Design Rationale
Flex is simpler than full 2D grid but handles 90% of TUI layouts. Constraint types mirror Ratatui's proven model. Solving algorithm is deterministic for predictable layouts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Flex struct with direction and items
- [ ] Full constraint type enum
- [ ] Deterministic constraint solver
- [ ] Gap/margin support
- [ ] Cross-axis alignment
- [ ] Nested Flex support
- [ ] Property tests for constraint invariants
- [ ] Edge case handling (overflow, zero space)

## Performance Budget
- Layout solve for 20 items: < 100µs
- No allocations for typical layouts (stack buffer)
